codeReview.summary: The hard-cut mostly unifies dealer settlement onto the payment-event core, but it regresses the public dealer auto-settle route when callers omit both referenceNumber and idempotencyKey.

issues.description: `autoSettleDealer` now forwards a `PartnerSettlementRequest` with `allocations = null` into `settleDealerInvoices` (DealerSettlementService:539-556). When the caller omits both `referenceNumber` and `idempotencyKey`, dealer-settlement idempotency falls back to `buildDealerSettlementIdempotencyKey()` (SettlementReferenceService:49-59,220-246), whose fingerprint only includes dealer/cashAccount/amount until allocations exist. The pre-hard-cut path built the auto-settle allocations before deriving the receipt identity, so different oldest-open invoice sets produced different implicit references. After this change, two legitimate auto-settle calls for the same dealer/account/amount can collide and replay the first journal instead of settling the next oldest invoice, breaking the existing public auto-settle contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation coverage for dealer auto-settle only exercised requests with explicit `referenceNumber` and `idempotencyKey`, so it missed the default keyless auto-settle path that used to derive identity from resolved allocations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: erp-domain/src/test/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/codered/CR_DealerReceiptSettlementAuditTrailTest.java:783-790 creates the new auto-settle request with both explicit fields populated; the handoff's manual validation notes auto-settle success but does not mention the no-reference/no-idempotency route.

summary: Fail: dealer settlements are moved onto the payment-event core, but dealer auto-settle now reuses the same implicit idempotency/reference for repeated same-amount calls without explicit keys, so later legitimate auto-settlements can replay the first posting instead of allocating the next oldest invoice.
